background.experience: remote field repair under intermittent connectivity

readers.thesis: A CI system becomes sovereign when a damaged worker can disappear without taking authority, evidence, or recovery knowledge with it.
observations: The conversation repeatedly converts provider failures into authority-boundary questions.
The current program requires executable proof rather than infrastructure branding.
uncertainties: The available corpus does not include every historical turn or private archive record.
The second-operator recovery capability remains unexecuted.

readers.thesis: Owning runners adds value only when policy, artifact acceptance, and exit paths remain independent of the scheduler, runtime, and physical host.
observations: Physical hardware custody and software control-plane ownership solve different failure classes.
Persistent runners create hidden cross-job state and single-provider assumptions.
uncertainties: Measured workload and concurrency data are absent.
Kernel and firmware compromise remain outside the policy prototype.

agreements: Workers contain no canonical state and receive no release authority.
Single-use execution narrows persistence and credential reuse.
Foundation retains job admission and artifact acceptance.
disagreements: Reader A prioritizes disposable virtual-machine isolation for hostile jobs; Reader B delays that cost until a measured risk tier requires it.
Reader A treats second-operator recovery as an immediate gate; Reader B treats it as a staged operational test.
omissions_detected: No measured boot-time, throughput, energy, or bandwidth benchmark exists.
No destructive worker-host laboratory has run.
No complete private corpus was available.
revisions_after_discussion: Adopt rootless containers for controlled internal jobs and require disposable virtual machines for untrusted public contributions.
Make second-operator blank-host reconstruction part of the first laboratory rather than a later documentation task.

notes_added.statement: Treat every CI worker as disposable hostile compute; preserve authority and canonical state outside the worker.
Prove worker sovereignty by destroying and rebuilding the fleet while retaining verifiable evidence and release separation.

notes_challenged.challenge: Immutable release digests remain necessary but do not prevent a compromised persistent worker from fabricating build evidence.

privacy.review: Contains only abstract capability conclusions, synthetic role descriptions, and non-identifying coverage boundaries. It excludes transcript excerpts, personal facts, health or animal details, credentials, locations, deleted text, and unique conversation fragments.
